verses.text: José começou como um escravo, alienado de seus irmãos e separado de seu pai; ele terminou como vice-rei do Faraó. Através das viagens ao Egito, a família do pacto passou da beira da apostasia, dividida por ciúmes e enganos, para ser reconciliada e unida pela intercessão de Judá e pelo perdão de José.\n\n

Deus é mencionado pela primeira vez (além de **38.7-10** ) desde que Jacó construiu seu altar em Betel ( **35.1-15** ); seu nome de aliança, “o Senhor,” é usado pela primeira vez desde que Jacó deixou Labão ( **31.49** ).

39:3 his master saw that the Lord was with him—Though changed in condition, Joseph was not changed in spirit; though stripped of the gaudy coat that had adorned his person, he had not lost the moral graces that distinguished his character; though separated from his father on earth, he still lived in communion with his Father in heaven; though in the house of an idolater, he continued a worshipper of the true God.

39:4 He made him overseer - הפקיד hiphkid, from פקד pakad, to visit, take care of, superintend; the same as επισκοπος, overseer or bishop, among the Greeks. This is the term by which the Septuagint often express the meaning of the original.
Deus começou a abençoar o Egito através de José (veja **22.18** ).

José começou como um escravo, alienado de seus irmãos e separado de seu pai; ele terminou como vice-rei do Faraó. Através das viagens ao Egito, a família do pacto passou da beira da apostasia, dividida por ciúmes e enganos, para ser reconciliada e unida pela intercessão de Judá e pelo perdão de José.\n\n

39:11 And it came to pass about this time,.... About a week, or a month, or rather a year, as Aben Ezra thinks, after she first began her solicitations to him. According to Josephus (r), it was a public festival, at which women used to attend; but she excused herself, pretending illness; and so Jarchi takes it to be some noted day at the idol's temple, to which all used to go; but she pretended she was sick, and could not go, knowing that Joseph would not be there, but at home, and therefore judged this a fit opportunity to attack him once more: and so it was: that Joseph went into the house to do his business: to inspect the accounts, as the Targums of Onkelos and Jonathan paraphrase it,…

39:12 And she caught him by his garment, saying, lie with me,.... It is very probable that before this time, as soon as ever she began to speak to him, he got away as fast as he could, and would not hear her; wherefore she now laid hold on his garment, in order to detain him, that she might have time to parley with him, and so prevail: and he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out;

39:13 And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand,.... And so all hopes of succeeding in her addresses to him were over: and he was fled forth; into the streets, or into some out house, where business was carried on by servants under him.

Embora a esposa de Potifar estivesse se dirigindo aos escravos, ela apelou a eles como compatriotas egípcios (nós) para recrutá-los como testemunhas contra o desprezado hebreu (veja **43.32** ) que havia conquistado a confiança de Potifar.

39:15 And it came to pass, when he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried,.... He attempting to ravish her, as she would have it understood, but afraid, lest upon her outcry those that were in the house should come in to her assistance, and seize on him: that he left his garment with me, and fled, and got him out: but why should he strip himself of his garment, and leave that behind him? he might have fled with it.

39:16 And she laid up his garment by her,.... As a proof of what she laid to his charge, and as a testimony against him: until her lord came home; or until his lord came home, for the pronoun refers to Joseph, and so Jarchi interprets it; who either was gone a journey, or gone to court that day, being an officer of Pharaoh's, or to the public place where the festival was kept that day, if it was such an one.

39:17 And she spake unto him according to these words,.... When her husband came home she related to him the above affair, according to the purport of the above words, and in much the same manner, and to the same import as she had to the men of her house: saying, the Hebrew servant which thou hast brought unto us came in unto me to mock me; in an obscene manner, using filthy words and actions, contrary to the rules of chastity as well as good manners;

39:18 And it came to pass, as I lifted up my voice and cried,.... For help from the servants, and frightened at his insolent attempt: that he left his garment with me, and fled out; and then she brought it forth, and showed him it.
Esta foi a segunda vez que José, enquanto fielmente fazia a coisa certa, foi jogado na escravidão com suas roupas usadas de forma enganosa como evidência (cp. **37.23-24** , **31-33** ).

José prosperou na prisão porque Deus estava com ele. Cada vez que José prosperava, ele era colocado no comando de algo.
